rulings.Case Title: GLORIA LEE ET AL VS. MEGABUS WEST, LLC, DBA MEGABUS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Farmers Insurance Exchange Notice Of Motion For Leave To Intervene
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 4, 2019, LINE 2, Motion 2 of 2 DEFENDANT FARMERS INSURANCE EXCHANGE Motion For Leave To Intervene GRANTED. No opposition filed. "The purpose of intervention is to promote fairness by involving all parties potentially affected by a judgment in the litigation." (Simpson Redwood Co. v. State of California (1987) 196 Cal.App.3d 1192, 1199 [citation omitted].) As an insurer for Defendant Yasinia Kennedy, Farmers has a direct interest in this litigation and a sound basis to intervene to defend against default on Ms. Kennedy's behalf under Code of Civil Procedure section 387 and Insurance Code section 11580. "[W]here the insurer may be subject to a direct action under Insurance Code section 11580 by a judgment creditor who has or will obtain a default judgment in a third party action against the insured, intervention is appropriate." (Reliance Insurance Company v. Superior Court (2002) 84 Cal.App.4th 383, 386-87.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Farmers is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. = EPS

rulings.Case Title: GLORIA LEE ET AL VS. MEGABUS WEST, LLC, DBA MEGABUS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Plaintiffs Service By Publication Of Defendant Yasinia Kennedy
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 4, 2019, LINE 2, Motion 1 of 2 DEFENDANT YASINIA KENNEDY'S Motion To Quash Plaintiffs Service By Publication Intervenor Farmers Insurance Exchange's motion to quash Plaintiffs Gloria Lee and David Greenberg's service by publication of Defendant Yasinia Kennedy is denied. In its October 12, 2018 Order, the Court found that Defendant Yasinia Kennedy could not be served with reasonable diligence by any means other than publication. (Code Civ. Proc. § 415.50(a)(1).) The Order authorized Plaintiffs to serve Ms. Kennedy by publication in the East Bay Express Newspaper and in the Las Vegas Review Journal. Intervenor has not provided any basis for the Court to question that prior order. (See also Greene v. State Farm Fire & Casualty Co. (1990) 224 Cal.App.3d 1583, 1588 [the power of one judge to vacate an order duly made by another judge is limited].) Thus, Plaintiffs properly served Ms. Kennedy by publication as authorized by the October 12, 2018 Order. In opposing Intervenor's motion, Plaintiffs have provided evidence that they published the service in each of these publications once a week for four successive weeks. (Albini Decl., Exhs. B, C.) They have, therefore, complied with the Order and with the requirements for service by publication under Code of Civil Procedure section 415.50. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. = EPS

rulings.Case Title: JACK H REISNER VS. UNITED BROTHERHOOD OF CARPENTERS AND JOINERS OF AM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 4, 2019, LINE 3 CROSS DEFENDANT ADAMS & ASSOCIATES, INC.'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings GRANTED without leave to amend as to the indemnity and declaratory relief causes of action. Plaintiff Reisner alleges employment claims that resulted in his termination. Those claims are a creature of an employment relationship. There are no allegations in the cross-complaint that Adams had an employment relationship with Plaintiff Reisner. Because no such relationship is alleged, Adams cannot be a joint tortfeasor and UBC cannot plead a claim for equitable indemnity. "Where multiple tortfeasors are responsible for an indivisible injury suffered by the plaintiff, each tortfeasor is jointly and severally liable to the plaintiff for those damages and thus may be held individually liable to the injured plaintiff for the entirety of such damages." (Expressions at Rancho Niguel Ass'n v. Ahmanson Developments, Inc. (2001) 86 Cal.App.4th 1135, 1139.) As a matter of law, Adams cannot be considered a joint tortfeasor because it was not Reisner's employer. "As a matter of law, only an employer can be liable for the tort of wrongful discharge in violation of public policy." (Khajavi v. Feather River Anesthesia Medical Group (2000) 84 Cal.App.4th 32, 53; accord, Miklosy v. Regents of University of California (2008) 44 Cal.4th 876, 898-901 [common law cause of action for wrongful termination in violation of public policy lies only against an employer].) Likewise, "the employer is liable for retaliation under section 12940, subdivision (h), but nonemployer individuals are not personally liable for their role in that retaliation." (Jones v. Lodge at Torrey Pines Partnership (2008) 42 Cal.4th 1158, 1173; see also Weinbaum v. Goldfarb, Whitman & Cohen (1996) 46 Cal.App.4th 1310, 1315 [nonemployer could not be liable for conspiracy to wrongfully discharge employee].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Adams is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. = EPS

rulings.Case Title: VINCE MORECI VS. FULTON STREET CONSTRUCTION INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Protective Order To Motion To Independent Medical Evaluation; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 4, 2019, Line 1, DEFENDANT FULTON STREET CONSTRUCTION, INC., ERIK LIU's Motion For Protective Order To Motion To Independent Medical Evaluation; Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Roger Mead, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted as follows: Plaintiff shall make himself available on a mutually convenient date within the next three weeks for a medical examination by Dr. Jadali. In conducting the examination Dr. Jadali will not be precluded from asking normal medical questions with regard to the plaintiff's medical history, including the medical history and treatment of plaintiff with regard to the subject injuries, prior injuries to the same body parts and pre-existing injuries to the same body parts claimed in the subject incident, but shall not include a medical history with regard to unrelated injuries to other body parts. Dr. Jadali may question plaintiff with regard to how he allegedly "slipped, tripped and/or fell on scaffolding stairs" from the time that he began falling and thereafter. The area of inquiry shall not include questions about the facts of the incident prior to the beginning of the alleged fall. Abel v. 1979 Union St. Corp., CGC-15-543471 (decided May 31, 2016). As in Abel, it would have been preferable if defendant had sought resolution of the dispute before the initial medical examination, and for that reason defendant's request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rmead@folgerlevin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KATHERINE ROBERTS VS. SWANROCK INCORPORATED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Consolidate Case No. Cgc-18-569262 With Case No. Cgc-17-562223; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Marissa N. Acree In Support Thereof; And Proposed Order
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 4, 2019, LINE 4 DEFENDANT SWANROCK, INC. DBA SKYTECH SOLAR's Motion To Consolidate Case No. CGC-18-569262 With Case No. CGC-17-562223 GRANTED and case No. CGC-17-562223 shall be the lead case. Because the two actions pending before this court involve common questions of law and fact, they are consolidated for all purposes pursuant to Code Civ. Proc., § 1048. Both actions concern defendant Swanrock's installation of solar panels on the roof of plaintiff Katherine Roberts' building and the damage Ms. Roberts suffered as a result of the allegedly wrongful conduct of Swanrock related to this installation. The actions should be litigated as one to avoid unnecessary costs, inefficiencies, and the possibility of inconsistent rulings. Because of the subrogation doctrine, the insurer State Farm, which has partially compensated Ms. Roberts for her loss, and Ms. Roberts both have the right to seek recovery of damages from Swanrock. (Allstate Ins. Co. v. Mel Rapton, Inc. (2000) 77 Cal.App.4th 901, 908.) When as, here, State Farm has not sought to intervene in its insured's action but chose instead to file a separate action, Swanrock can properly move to consolidate the two actions. (See Hodge v. Kirkpatrick Development, Inc. (2005) 130 Cal.App.4th 540, 550.) Ms. Roberts' reliance on Evidence Code § 1155, which governs the admissibility of evidence at trial, is misplaced, and she cites no authority to support her argument that consolidation under such circumstances should not be granted because of possible prejudice to the plaintiff. Ms. Roberts' concern appears to be related to the application of the collateral source rule. However, the collateral source rule does not prevent an insurer from recovering from the third party tortfeasor in subrogation. (See Garbell v. Conejo Hardwoods, Inc. (2011) 193 Cal.App.4th 1563, 1572.) Any concerns regarding the admissibility of evidence at trial concerning State Farm's identity or partial payments to Ms. Roberts may be addressed in pretrial motions in limine. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Swanrock is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. = EPS
